AgentContext.description:
Result of agent-context detection at process start.

When `detected == true`, the CLI layer should (per §1.5) auto-enable
`--json`, `--log=json`, `--no-color`, `--no-progress`, `--no-interactive`.
Explicit user flags win over these defaults.

name.description:
The value of the detected env var (e.g. `"goose"` for `AGENT=goose`).
For bool-style markers (`CLAUDECODE=1`) this is the raw string.

source.description: Which env var surfaced the agent (present iff `detected`).

AgentSource.description:
Which env var triggered agent detection. Recorded in `akua whoami`
output for introspection and in debug-level logs for post-hoc diagnosis.

oneOf.description: Generic `AGENT=<name>` variable (Goose, Amp, Codex, Cline, OpenCode).

error.description: One-line error from the failing check; `null` when `ok`.

issues.description:
Per-file issues from linting the Package.k. Other check kinds
leave this empty. Always serialized (possibly empty) so
consumers can iterate without a presence guard.

ExitCode.description:
The seven typed exit codes every akua verb may produce.

Agents branch on these. Humans read them. The stability contract is
that meanings never change; new codes require a major version bump.

Serialized as the stable kebab-case name (`"success"`, `"user-error"`,
`"policy-deny"`, etc.) — same string [`ExitCode::name`] returns. The
SDK maps from the numeric `child.exitCode` (0..=6) to this name.

oneOf.description:
`1` — invalid inputs, bad flags, missing required arguments.
Something the caller got wrong.

`2` — unexpected failure (disk, network, bug). Something akua
couldn't recover from that isn't the caller's fault.
`3` — the policy engine rejected the operation.
`4` — registry / API rate limits. Retry with backoff.

`5` — the operation is allowed but requires human approval
before it can proceed.

changed.description:
True when the file's contents differ from the formatter's
output. Under `--check`, this flips the exit code to
[`ExitCode::UserError`]; without `--check`, the file is
rewritten in place.

Level.description: Log severity. Almost always `Error`; `Warn` for recoverable issues.

LintIssue.description:
Structured issue reported by [`lint_kcl`]. Mirrors KCL's own
`Error.messages[*]` shape, flattened one-row-per-message so
consumers don't need to walk a two-level tree.

code.description:
KCL error code (e.g. `"E1001"`). Empty string when KCL emits no
code — preserved verbatim.

RenderSummary.description:
Verdict for a single render: where manifests went, how many, and a
hash that makes "did the rendered output change?" a byte comparison.

files.description:
Relative filenames, in write order. Empty vec is serialized as
omitted to keep the JSON shape tight.

hash.description:
`sha256:<hex>` of the concatenated `<filename>\n<yaml>` blocks.
Filename participates in the hash so two identical resources
routed to different filenames produce different hashes.

target.description:
Directory the manifests were written into (or would be, on
`--dry-run`). Absolute or relative depending on the `out_root`
the caller passed.

StructuredError.description:
The canonical error shape. One struct; every verb reuses it.

`code` is the stable, load-bearing identifier. `message` is the one-line
summary. The rest are optional pointers — a file path, a field name, a
suggestion, a docs URL.

docs.description:
URL to the error's documentation page. Convention:
`https://akua.dev/errors/<CODE>`.

field.description:
Dotted field path inside `path` when the error is field-specific.
Example: `spec.replicas`, `metadata.labels.team`.

level.description:
Log level. Always `"error"` for hard failures; `"warn"` for
recoverable issues emitted alongside a success exit.

next_actions.description:
Machine-executable recovery steps. Agents can use these to fix
the error programmatically. Each entry is a complete command.

path.description: File path, OCI ref, or resource identifier the error refers to.

suggestion.description:
Actionable suggestion — a one-liner the caller can apply.
Absent when there's no generic fix.

WhoamiOutput.description:
Whoami response shape. Part of the stability contract — new fields
may be added (backward-compatible); existing field semantics never
change.

description: Machine-readable shape of every type akua emits under `--json`. Bind any validator (ajv, Zod, pydantic, gojsonschema, ...) against this.
title: akua CLI contract
